scope: Aave V3 Pool mainnet 0x87870bca3f3fd6335c3f4ce8392d69350b4fa4e2 (100 tx) + Pool Arbitrum 0x794a61358d6845594f94dc1db02a252b5b4814ad (100 tx) + WrappedTokenGatewayV3 (WETHGateway) mainnet 0xd01607c3c5ecaba394d8be377a08590149325722 (100 tx). selector-agnostic txlist sort=desc. NOTE: Base (8453) and Optimism (10) are NOT pullable on the free Etherscan v2 key ('Free API access is not supported for this chain') — only chain 1 and 42161 were sampled.
command: set -a; source crates/integration-tests/.env; set +a ; curl 'https://api.etherscan.io/v2/api?chainid=<C>&module=account&action=txlist&address=<ADDR>&offset=100&sort=desc&apikey=$KEY' > /tmp/v3diag-aave/raw-*.json ; target/debug/v3-harness import-etherscan /tmp/v3diag-aave/raw-pool-42161.json --chain 42161 --out /tmp/v3diag-aave/aave/corpus.json ; target/debug/v3-harness corpus --root /tmp/v3diag-aave
_totals_note: totals reflect the placed representative corpus (data/golden/v3-decode/aave/corpus.json, dedup <=2/selector): 30 covered verbose-ABI entries expect pass/lending (12 distinct selectors across chain1 Pool/WETHGateway + chain42161 Pool), 5 L2Pool packed-args entries (3 distinct selectors) expect error/no_declarative_v3_mapper. corpus replay = 35/35 matched (isolation); within the full golden dir all 35 aave entries pass (the only golden MISSes are pre-existing hyperliquid entries, unrelated). RAW TRAFFIC observed: chain1 Pool 100/100 selectors covered; chain1 WETHGateway 100/100 covered; chain42161 Pool 69/100 covered (verbose-ABI) + 31/100 UNCOVERED L2Pool packed (withdraw(bytes32) 23, repay(bytes32) 7, setUserUseReserveAsCollateral(bytes32) 1). 0 hard decode errors anywhere.

coverage_matrix.name: withdraw(bytes32)
coverage_matrix.note: UNCOVERED L2Pool packed-args. Highest uncovered traffic. asset = uint16 reserve-index (not address) packed in bytes32 word; needs onchain getReserveAddressById + bit-field emit strategy.

coverage_matrix.name: repay(bytes32)
coverage_matrix.note: UNCOVERED L2Pool packed-args. reserve-index asset + interestRateMode packed bits.

coverage_matrix.name: setUserUseReserveAsCollateral(bytes32)
coverage_matrix.note: UNCOVERED L2Pool packed-args. reserve-index + bool flag bit.

coverage_matrix.name: supply(bytes32)

traffic._observed: 0 in this sample; verbose supply 0x617ba037 dominant on Arbitrum top-100

coverage_matrix.note: UNCOVERED L2Pool packed-args (full IL2Pool surface; not in sample but is the L2 supply path)

coverage_matrix.name: rebalanceStableBorrowRate(bytes32)
coverage_matrix.note: UNCOVERED L2Pool packed-args (full surface; stable-rate path, mostly disabled post-2023)

coverage_matrix.note: UNCOVERED verbose-ABI. Integrator/MEV path, low retail frequency. Declarative single_emit feasible (no packing). High security relevance.

coverage_soft.detail: full IL2Pool surface, not in sample

coverage_soft.name: flashLoanSimple
coverage_soft.detail: verbose-ABI, 0 in sample but known uncovered surface

summary: Aave V3 mainnet (verbose-ABI Pool + WrappedTokenGatewayV3) is 100% covered for all sampled selectors: every selector in the chain1 Pool 100-tx sample and the chain1 WETHGateway 100-tx sample already has a manifest and decodes pass/lending — 0 hard. The headline coverage gap is the entire Aave V3 L2Pool packed-args interface (IL2Pool, ~12 functions, packed bytes32 calldata via CalldataLogic) used by the Arbitrum/Base/Optimism UIs: on the chain42161 Pool 100-tx run, 31/100 txs hit uncovered L2Pool selectors (withdraw(bytes32) 23, repay(bytes32) 7, setUserUseReserveAsCollateral(bytes32) 1) and all decode soft/no_declarative_v3_mapper. CRITICAL CONSTRAINT making this NON-trivial: L2Pool packs the asset as a uint16 reserve-list INDEX (not an address), plus amount/rateMode/collateral-flag as bit-fields inside the bytes32 word — so asset.key.address cannot be filled from calldata alone (requires onchain getReserveAddressById(uint16) via live_inputs) and the current declarative emit DSL has no bit-field/packed-word strategy (strategy.rs explicitly notes none exists). Therefore L2Pool needs a new packed-word/bit-slice emit strategy or a Tier B wrapper, NOT a copy-paste single_emit manifest. Same proxy address as the verbose Pool, so chain_to_addresses is identical to the existing aave/v3 manifests. SECONDARY gap: flashLoan(0xab9c4b5d) + flashLoanSimple(0x42b0b77c) are uncovered verbose-ABI functions (0 in the samples, integrator path) — declaratively feasible and high security relevance. IMPORTANT SCOPE NOTE: Base (8453) and Optimism (10) could NOT be sampled — the free Etherscan v2 key rejects those chains ('Free API access is not supported for this chain'); only chain 1 and 42161 were observable. Base/Optimism organic verbose-vs-L2 split is therefore unmeasured here, but L2Pool is the standard L2 entrypoint so the gap generalizes.
next: PRIMARY: add Aave V3 L2Pool packed-args coverage. Blocked on pure-declarative: needs (a) a new emit strategy that bit-slices a bytes32 arg (assetId u16 | amount u128 | rateMode/refCode bits), AND (b) reserve-index->address resolution via live_inputs onchain_view getReserveAddressById(uint16). Likely Tier B. Same chain_to_addresses as existing aave/v3 manifests (L2Pool == Pool proxy on L2 chains).
SECONDARY: add flashLoan(0xab9c4b5d) + flashLoanSimple(0x42b0b77c) manifests (verbose ABI, declarative single_emit feasible) — uncovered, low traffic, high security relevance.
Base(8453)/Optimism(10) need a paid Etherscan plan or Dune to characterize organic verbose-vs-L2 split; not pullable on the free key.
